children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (i), EBT cards shall be issued only to households which have been duly certified as eligible to participate in the supplemental nutrition assistance program.

children.heading: Use
children.label: b Use
children.text: Benefits issued to eligible households shall be used by them only to purchase food from retail food stores which have been approved for participation in the supplemental nutrition assistance program at prices prevailing in such stores: , That nothing in this chapter shall be construed as authorizing the Secretary to specify the prices at which food may be sold by wholesale food concerns or retail food stores.Provided

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: EBT cards issued to eligible households shall be simple in design and shall include only such words or illustrations as are required to explain their purpose.

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: 2 Prohibition
children.text: The name of any public official shall not appear on any EBT card.

children.heading: Delivery and control procedures
children.label: d Delivery and control procedures
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe appropriate procedures for the delivery of benefits to benefit issuers and for the subsequent controls to be placed over such benefits by benefit issuers in order to ensure adequate accountability.

children.heading: State issuance liability
children.label: e State issuance liability
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this chapter, the State agency shall be strictly liable to the Secretary for any financial losses involved in the acceptance, storage and issuance of benefits, except that in the case of losses resulting from the issuance and replacement of authorizations for benefits which are sent through the mail, the State agency shall be liable to the Secretary to the extent prescribed in the regulations promulgated by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: If the Secretary determines, in consultation with the Inspector General of the Department of Agriculture, that it would improve the integrity of the supplemental nutrition assistance program, the Secretary shall require a State agency to issue or deliver benefits using alternative methods.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the Secretary shall require participating retail food stores (including restaurants participating in a State option restaurant program intended to serve the elderly, disabled, and homeless) to pay 100 percent of the costs of acquiring, and arrange for the implementation of, electronic benefit transfer point-of-sale equipment and supplies, including related services.

children.label: i
children.text: farmers’ markets and other direct-to-consumer markets, military commissaries, nonprofit food buying cooperatives, and establishments, organizations, programs, or group living arrangements described in paragraphs (5), (7), and (8) of ; andsection 2012(k) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: establishments described in paragraphs (3), (4), and (9) of , other than restaurants participating in a State option restaurant program.section 2012(k) of this title

children.heading: Interchange fees
children.label: C Interchange fees
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph permits the charging of fees relating to the redemption of supplemental nutrition assistance program benefits, in accordance with subsection (h)(13).

children.heading: Devaluation and termination of issuance of paper coupons
children.label: 3 Devaluation and termination of issuance of paper coupons

children.heading: Coupon issuance
children.label: A Coupon issuance
children.text: Effective on the date of enactment of the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008, no State shall issue any coupon, stamp, certificate, or authorization card to a household that receives supplemental nutrition assistance under this chapter.

children.heading: EBT cards
children.label: B EBT cards
children.text: Effective beginning on the date that is 1 year after the date of enactment of the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008, only an EBT card issued under subsection (i) shall be eligible for exchange at any retail food store.

children.label: i
children.text: no longer be an obligation of the Federal Government; and

children.label: ii
children.text: not be redeemable.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Effective beginning on , except as provided in subparagraph (B), no State shall issue manual vouchers to a household that receives supplemental nutrition assistance under this chapter or allow retail food stores to accept manual vouchers as payment, unless the Secretary determines that the manual vouchers are necessary, such as in the event of an electronic benefit transfer system failure or a disaster situation.February 7, 2014

children.heading: Exemptions
children.label: B Exemptions
children.text: The Secretary may exempt categories of retail food stores or individual retail food stores from subparagraph (A) based on criteria established by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: To enhance the anti-fraud protections of the program, the Secretary shall require all parties providing electronic benefit transfer services to provide for and maintain unique terminal identification number information through the supplemental nutrition assistance program electronic benefit transfer transaction routing system.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Not earlier than 2 years after , the Secretary shall issue proposed regulations to carry out this paragraph.February 7, 2014

children.heading: Commercial practices
children.label: ii Commercial practices
children.text: In issuing regulations to carry out this paragraph, the Secretary shall consider existing commercial practices for other point-of-sale debit transactions.

children.label: C Operation of individual point of sale device by farmers’ markets and direct marketing farmers

children.label: II
children.text: if the Secretary determines that the technology is not available for a point of sale device to meet the requirement under subclause (I), the farmers’ market or direct marketing farmer provides to the Secretary any other information, as determined by the Secretary, necessary to ensure the integrity of transactions processed using the point of sale device.

The State agency may establish a procedure for staggering the issuance of benefits to eligible households throughout the month. Upon the request of the tribal organization that exercises governmental jurisdiction over the reservation, the State agency shall stagger the issuance of benefits for eligible households located on reservations for at least 15 days of a month.

children.heading: .—Multiple issuances
children.label: B .—Multiple issuances
children.text: The procedure may include issuing benefits to a household in more than 1 issuance during a month only when a benefit correction is necessary.

children.heading: .—Implementation
children.label: A .—Implementation
children.text: Not later than , each State agency shall implement an electronic benefit transfer system under which household benefits determined under section 2017(a) or 2035 of this title are issued from and stored in a central databank, unless the Secretary provides a waiver for a State agency that faces unusual barriers to implementing an electronic benefit transfer system.October 1, 2002

children.heading: .—Timely implementation
children.label: B .—Timely implementation
children.text: Each State agency is encouraged to implement an electronic benefit transfer system under subparagraph (A) as soon as practicable.

children.heading: .—State flexibility
children.label: C .—State flexibility
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), a State agency may procure and implement an electronic benefit transfer system under the terms, conditions, and design that the State agency considers appropriate.

The Secretary shall periodically inform State agencies of the advantages of using electronic benefit systems to issue benefits in accordance with this subsection in lieu of issuing coupons to households.
This subsection shall not diminish the authority of the Secretary to conduct projects to test automated or electronic benefit delivery systems under .section 2026(f) of this title

children.heading: .—Replacement of benefits
children.label: 7 .—Replacement of benefits
children.text: Regulations issued by the Secretary regarding the replacement of benefits and liability for replacement of benefits under an electronic benefit transfer system shall be similar to the regulations in effect for a paper-based supplemental nutrition assistance issuance system.

children.heading: .—Fees
children.label: A .—Fees
children.text: A State agency may collect a charge for replacement of an electronic benefit transfer card by reducing the monthly allotment of the household receiving the replacement card.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Subject to terms and conditions established by the Secretary in accordance with clause (ii), if a household makes excessive requests for replacement of the electronic benefit transfer card of the household, the Secretary may require a State agency to decline to issue a replacement card to the household unless the household, upon request of the State agency, provides an explanation for the loss of the card.

children.label: I
children.text: the household be given the opportunity to provide the requested explanation and meet the requirements under this paragraph promptly;

children.label: II
children.text: after an excessive number of lost cards, the head of the household shall be required to review program rights and responsibilities with State agency personnel authorized to make determinations under ; andsection 2014(a) of this title

children.label: III
children.text: any action taken, including actions required under , other than the withholding of the electronic benefit transfer card until an explanation described in subclause (I) is provided, shall be consistent with the due process protections under section 2015(b) or 2020(e)(10) of this title, as appropriate.section 2015(b)(2) of this title

children.heading: .—Protecting vulnerable persons
children.label: C .—Protecting vulnerable persons
children.text: In implementing this paragraph, a State agency shall act to protect homeless persons, persons with disabilities, victims of crimes, and other vulnerable persons who lose electronic benefit transfer cards but are not intentionally committing fraud.

children.heading: .—Effect on eligibility
children.label: D .—Effect on eligibility
children.text: While a State may decline to issue an electronic benefits transfer card until a household satisfies the requirements under this paragraph, nothing in this paragraph shall be considered a denial of, or limitation on, the eligibility for benefits under .section 2014 of this title

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: A State agency may require that an electronic benefit card contain a photograph of 1 or more members of a household.

children.heading: .—Other authorized users
children.label: B .—Other authorized users
children.text: If a State agency requires a photograph on an electronic benefit card under subparagraph (A), the State agency shall establish procedures to ensure that any other appropriate member of the household or any authorized representative of the household may utilize the card.

children.heading: .—Federal law not applicable
children.label: 10 .—Federal law not applicable
children.text: Section 1693–2 of title 15 shall not apply to electronic benefit transfer or reimbursement systems under this chapter.o

children.label: 11 Application of anti-tying restrictions to electronic benefit transfer systems.—

children.label: i .—Affiliate
children.text: The term “affiliate” has the meaning provided the term in .section 1841(k) of title 12

children.heading: .—Company
children.label: ii .—Company
children.text: The term “company” has the meaning provided the term in , but shall not include a bank, a bank holding company, or any subsidiary of a bank holding company.section 1971 of title 12

children.text: The term “point-of-sale service” means any product or service related to the electronic authorization and processing of payments for merchandise at a retail food store, including credit or debit card services, automated teller machines, point-of-sale terminals, or access to on-line systems.

children.heading: .—Consultation with the federal reserve board
children.label: C .—Consultation with the federal reserve board
children.text: Before promulgating regulations or interpretations of regulations to carry out this paragraph, the Secretary shall consult with the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: A State agency shall establish a procedure for recovering electronic benefits from the account of a household due to inactivity, or due to the death of all members of the household.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: A State agency may store recovered electronic benefits off-line in accordance with clause (ii), if the household has not accessed the account after 3 months.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), a State agency shall expunge benefits that have not been accessed by a household after a period of 9 months, or upon verification that all members of the household are deceased.

children.label: I

children.heading: .—Interchange fees
children.label: A .—Interchange fees
children.text: No interchange fees shall apply to electronic benefit transfer transactions under this subsection.

children.heading: .—Other fees
children.label: B .—Other fees
children.text: Effective through fiscal year 2023, neither a State, nor any agent, contractor, or subcontractor of a State who facilitates the provision of supplemental nutrition assistance program benefits in such State may impose a fee for switching (as defined in subsection (j)(1)(H)) or routing such benefits.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the Secretary shall authorize the use of mobile technologies for the purpose of accessing supplemental nutrition assistance program benefits.

children.heading: State option to issue benefits to certain individuals made ineligible by welfare reform
children.label: i State option to issue benefits to certain individuals made ineligible by welfare reform

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, a State agency may, with the approval of the Secretary, issue benefits under this chapter to an individual who is ineligible to participate in the supplemental nutrition assistance program solely as a result of section 2015()(2) of this title or section 1612 or 1613 of title 8.o

children.label: i

children.heading: Crediting
children.label: B Crediting
children.text: Notwithstanding , payments received under subparagraph (A) shall be credited to the supplemental nutrition assistance program appropriation account or the account from which the costs were drawn, as appropriate, for the fiscal year in which the payment is received.section 3302(b) of title 31

children.heading: Reporting
children.label: 3 Reporting
children.text: To be eligible to issue benefits under this subsection, a State agency shall comply with reporting requirements established by the Secretary to carry out this subsection.

children.label: A

children.label: B
children.text: obtain the approval of the Secretary for the plan.

children.heading: Violations
children.label: 5 Violations
children.text: A sanction, disqualification, fine, or other penalty prescribed under Federal law (including sections 2021 and 2024 of this title) shall apply to a violation committed in connection with a benefit issued under this subsection.

children.heading: Ineligibility for administrative reimbursement
children.label: 6 Ineligibility for administrative reimbursement
children.text: Administrative and other costs incurred in issuing a benefit under this subsection shall not be eligible for Federal funding under this chapter.

children.heading: Exclusion from enhanced payment accuracy systems
children.label: 7 Exclusion from enhanced payment accuracy systems
children.text: shall not apply to benefits issued under this subsection.Section 2025(c) of this title

children.heading: Interoperability and portability of electronic benefit transfer transactions
children.label: j Interoperability and portability of electronic benefit transfer transactions

children.heading: Electronic benefit transfer card
children.label: A Electronic benefit transfer card
children.text: The term “electronic benefit transfer card” means a card that provides benefits under this chapter through an electronic benefit transfer service (as defined in subsection (h)(11)(A)).

children.heading: Electronic benefit transfer contract
children.label: B Electronic benefit transfer contract
children.text: The term “electronic benefit transfer contract” means a contract that provides for the issuance, use, or redemption of program benefits in the form of electronic benefit transfer cards.

children.heading: Interoperability
children.label: C Interoperability
children.text: The term “interoperability” means a system that enables program benefits in the form of an electronic benefit transfer card to be redeemed in any State.

children.heading: Interstate transaction
children.label: D Interstate transaction
children.text: The term “interstate transaction” means a transaction that is initiated in 1 State by the use of an electronic benefit transfer card that is issued in another State.

children.heading: Portability
children.label: E Portability
children.text: The term “portability” means a system that enables program benefits in the form of an electronic benefit transfer card to be used in any State by a household to purchase food at a retail food store or wholesale food concern approved under this chapter.

children.heading: Settling
children.label: F Settling
children.text: The term “settling” means movement, and reporting such movement, of funds from an electronic benefit transfer card issuer that is located in 1 State to a retail food store, or wholesale food concern, that is located in another State, to accomplish an interstate transaction.

children.heading: Smart card
children.label: G Smart card
children.text: The term “smart card” means an intelligent benefit card described in .section 2026(f) of this title

children.heading: Switching
children.label: H Switching
children.text: The term “switching” means the routing of an intrastate or interstate transaction that consists of transmitting the details of a transaction electronically recorded through the use of an electronic benefit transfer card in one State to the issuer of the card that may be in the same or different State.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: 2 Requirement
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall ensure that systems that provide for the electronic issuance, use, and redemption of program benefits in the form of electronic benefit transfer cards are interoperable, and supplemental nutrition assistance program benefits are portable, among all States.October 1, 2002

children.heading: Cost
children.label: 3 Cost
children.text: The cost of achieving the interoperability and portability required under paragraph (2) shall not be imposed on any retail store, or any wholesale food concern, approved to participate in the supplemental nutrition assistance program.

children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (4), the Secretary shall approve retail food stores to accept benefits from recipients of supplemental nutrition assistance through on-line transactions.

children.label: 3 State agency action
children.text: Each State agency shall ensure that recipients of supplemental nutrition assistance can use benefits on-line as described in this subsection as appropriate.

children.heading: Demonstration project on acceptance of benefits through on-line transactions
children.label: 4 Demonstration project on acceptance of benefits through on-line transactions

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Before the Secretary authorizes implementation of paragraph (1) in all States, the Secretary shall carry out a number of demonstration projects as determined by the Secretary to test the feasibility of allowing retail food stores to accept benefits through on-line transactions.

children.label: i
children.text: a method of ensuring that benefits may be used to purchase only eligible items under this chapter;

children.label: ii
children.text: a description of the method of educating participant households about the availability and operation of on-line purchasing;

children.label: iii
children.text: adequate testing of the on-line purchasing option prior to implementation;

children.label: iv
children.text: the provision of data as requested by the Secretary for purposes of analyzing the impact of the project on participant access, ease of use, and program integrity;

children.label: v
children.text: reports on progress, challenges, and results, as determined by the Secretary; and

children.label: vi
children.text: such other criteria, including security criteria, as established by the Secretary.
